title: Build CSE Roadmap Tracker with Authentication, Dashboard, Timeline, and Progress Tracking

requirements.text: Implement Internet Identity authentication system with login, logout, and protected routes that auto-redirect unauthenticated users

quotes: Authentication System - Login - Register - Logout - JWT token storage - Protected routes - Auto redirect if not authenticated

acceptanceCriteria: Users can authenticate using Internet Identity
Protected routes redirect to login when user is not authenticated
Authentication state persists across page refreshes
Logout functionality clears authentication and redirects to login

requirements.text: Create Dashboard page with welcome message displaying user name, overview cards showing Total Tasks, Completed Tasks, Pending Tasks, and Current Streak, plus a progress bar

quotes: Dashboard - Welcome message with user name - Overview cards: Total Tasks, Completed Tasks, Pending Tasks, Current Streak - Progress bar

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays personalized welcome message with user's name
Four overview cards show real-time task statistics and streak data
Progress bar visualizes overall completion percentage
All metrics update dynamically based on task data

requirements.text: Add Recharts analytics to Dashboard with Weekly Progress Chart showing task completion over time and Completion Pie Chart displaying completed vs pending tasks

quotes: Charts using Recharts: - Weekly Progress Chart - Completion Pie Chart

acceptanceCriteria: Weekly Progress Chart displays task completion data for the last 7 days
Pie Chart shows proportion of completed vs pending tasks
Charts are interactive and responsive
Data updates automatically when tasks are modified

requirements.text: Build Timeline view with collapsible accordion structure organized by Month → Week → Day, allowing users to track daily progress and mark topics as complete

quotes: Timeline (Month → Week → Day view) - Collapsible accordion structure - Track daily progress - Mark topics complete

acceptanceCriteria: Timeline displays hierarchical month/week/day structure with accordion navigation
Users can expand/collapse each level independently
Daily topics can be marked as complete with persistent state
Visual indicators show completion status at each level

requirements.text: Implement Daily Planner with full CRUD operations: add, edit, delete tasks, mark as complete, set due dates, and assign priority levels (High, Medium, Low)

quotes: Daily Planner - Add Task - Edit Task - Delete Task - Mark Complete - Due Date - Priority (High, Medium, Low)

acceptanceCriteria: Users can create new tasks with title, description, due date, and priority
Tasks can be edited and deleted
Tasks can be toggled between complete and incomplete states
Tasks are filtered and sorted by date and priority
All changes persist to backend

requirements.text: Create Project Tracker with ability to add projects, update status (Planning, In Progress, Completed), include GitHub links, and list tech stack for each project

quotes: Project Tracker - Add project - Status (Planning, In Progress, Completed) - GitHub link - Tech stack

acceptanceCriteria: Users can create projects with name, description, status, GitHub link, and tech stack
Projects can be edited and deleted
Status can be updated between Planning, In Progress, and Completed
GitHub links are clickable and open in new tabs
Tech stack is displayed as tags or badges

requirements.text: Build Mock Test Tracker to add tests with name, score, and date, plus display improvement graph showing score trends over time

quotes: Mock Test Tracker - Add test name - Score - Date - Improvement graph

acceptanceCriteria: Users can add mock tests with test name, score, and date
Tests can be edited and deleted
Improvement graph displays score progression over time using Recharts
Graph shows clear trend line and data points for each test

requirements.text: Create Progress Tracking page displaying overall completion percentage, comprehensive graphs, and weekly performance trend analytics

quotes: Progress Tracking Page - Overall % completion - Graphs - Weekly performance trend

acceptanceCriteria: Page shows overall completion percentage prominently
Multiple charts visualize progress across different dimensions
Weekly performance trend chart shows task completion patterns
All metrics are calculated from actual user data

requirements.text: Implement backend User Profile module with get profile and update profile endpoints

quotes: User Module - Get profile - Update profile

acceptanceCriteria: Get profile endpoint returns authenticated user's profile data
Update profile endpoint allows modification of user information
Profile data includes name, email, and preferences
Changes persist to stable storage

requirements.text: Create backend Task module with endpoints to create, update, delete tasks, mark complete, and get all tasks filtered by authenticated user

quotes: Task Module - Create Task - Update Task - Delete Task - Mark Complete - Get all tasks by user

acceptanceCriteria: CRUD endpoints for tasks are fully functional
Tasks are scoped to authenticated user (user cannot see others' tasks)
Mark complete endpoint toggles task completion status
Get all tasks returns tasks sorted by due date and priority

requirements.text: Implement backend Project module with endpoints to add, update status, delete projects, and get all projects for authenticated user

quotes: Project Module - Add Project - Update Status - Delete Project - Get all projects

acceptanceCriteria: Projects can be created with all required fields
Status update endpoint accepts Planning, In Progress, and Completed values
Projects can be deleted
Get all projects returns user's projects only

requirements.text: Create backend Mock Test module with endpoints to add tests, update scores, get all tests, and provide performance analytics data

quotes: Mock Test Module - Add Test - Update Score - Get all tests - Performance analytics

acceptanceCriteria: Mock tests can be created with name, score, and date
Scores can be updated after creation
Get all tests returns tests sorted by date
Performance analytics endpoint calculates trends and improvement metrics

requirements.text: Implement backend Analytics API with endpoints returning total tasks, completed tasks, weekly progress data, and overall completion percentage

quotes: Analytics API - Total tasks - Completed tasks - Weekly progress - Completion percentage

acceptanceCriteria: Analytics endpoint returns real-time task statistics
Weekly progress data includes daily breakdown for last 7 days
Completion percentage calculated from total vs completed tasks
All metrics are user-specific and authenticated

requirements.text: Structure frontend with proper folder organization: components, pages, context, hooks, services, layouts, and routes directories with clean reusable components

quotes: Proper folder structure: src/ components/ pages/ context/ hooks/ services/ layouts/ routes/ - Clean reusable components

acceptanceCriteria: Frontend follows organized directory structure
Components are modular and reusable
Services abstract API calls
Hooks encapsulate shared logic
Clear separation of concerns throughout codebase

requirements.text: Implement comprehensive error handling with proper error states, loading states, and toast notifications for user feedback

quotes: Proper error handling - Loading states - Toast notifications

acceptanceCriteria: All API calls show loading states during requests
Error messages display clearly when operations fail
Success/error toast notifications appear for user actions
Loading spinners or skeletons shown during data fetching

requirements.text: Ensure full responsive design for both mobile and desktop viewports with clean, modern, professional UI inspired by Notion and Linear aesthetics

quotes: Clean modern professional UI (like Notion / Linear style)

acceptanceCriteria: Application is fully functional on mobile devices (320px+)
Desktop layout optimizes screen space (1024px+)
UI follows clean, minimal aesthetic with professional typography
Navigation adapts to screen size (hamburger menu on mobile)
All interactive elements are touch-friendly on mobile

requirements.text: Include example dummy data for tasks, projects, mock tests, and timeline entries to demonstrate full functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Backend includes seed data initialization for new users
Dummy data covers all major features (tasks, projects, tests, timeline)
Example data is realistic and representative of actual usage
Seed data can be easily cleared or regenerated

constraints: Use Motoko for backend instead of Spring Boot and MySQL
Use Internet Identity authentication instead of JWT
All backend data must persist in Motoko stable storage
Frontend must use React with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
Use Recharts library for all charts and analytics visualizations
Follow single-actor architecture for Motoko backend
nonGoals: Spring Boot or Java backend implementation
MySQL or any external database integration
Zustand state management (use React Context API)
Social authentication providers beyond Internet Identity
